notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description: We will remind you to log your mood regularly. Consistency is key to gaining insights and building awareness.
notifications.notifications_description: Regular reminders help you stay consistent and mindful.
notifications.notification_body: Take a moment to check in with yourself

cbt.cbt_definition: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) is a proven therapeutic approach that explores the connections between our thoughts, feelings, and behaviors.
cbt.about_description: Our thoughts strongly influence how we feel and how we act — often automatically and without us noticing. This exercise helps you pause when you notice difficult thoughts, look at a situation more clearly, and respond with intention instead of habit.
cbt.about_example: For example, noticing "I always mess things up" and asking "Is this thought helping me right now?"
cbt.step_1_situation: Briefly describe what happened, just the facts.
cbt.step_2_automatic_thought: Write the first thought that came to mind. Don't judge it.
cbt.step_3_behavior: Note what you did (or avoided doing) as a result of the thought.
cbt.step_4_emotions: Notice how this thought affected your feelings.
cbt.step_5_cognitive_distortions: Notice if this thought follows a common thinking pattern. Naming a distortion helps create distance from the thought.
cbt.step_6_alternative_thought: Gently explore a more balanced or helpful perspective. You don't need to be perfect — honesty matters more than wording.
cbt.awareness_goal: There's no "correct" answer — awareness is the goal.
cbt.reminder_text: This tool is for self-reflection and emotional awareness. It's not a replacement for professional mental health care.
cbt.situation_info: Describe what was happening when the thoughts or feelings started. Include the trigger if you can
cbt.situation_example: I had to introduce myself to a new team and felt rushed
cbt.automatic_thought_info: Write the first thought that came to your mind in that moment. Focus on the quick, immediate reaction you had
cbt.cognitive_distortions_info: Cognitive Distortions are common thinking patterns that can contribute to negative emotions. Select any that applied to your automatic thought
cbt.automatic_thought_example: I made a bad impression — they probably think I’m awkward or unprofessional
cbt.behavior_info: Describe what you did next in response to the thought. Note any actions, avoidance, or changes in how you interacted
cbt.behavior_example: I kept quiet for the rest of the meeting and avoided making eye contact
cbt.alternate_thought_info: Write a more balanced or realistic way of looking at the situation. Focus on facts, not assumptions
cbt.alternate_thought_example: I was a bit rushed, but introductions are brief and people understand that. One moment doesn’t define how the team sees me
cbt.emotions_info: List the emotions you felt during the situation. Try to be specific (e.g., anxiety, embarrassment, frustration)
cbt.alternative_thought_question: What could be an alternative, more balanced thought?
cbt.alternative_thought_hint: Consider other perspectives or interpretations of the situation
cbt.add_log: Add your first thought journal entry for today
cbt.black_and_white_thinking_desc: Viewing situations in black-and-white terms, without recognizing any middle ground
cbt.catostrophizing_desc: Expecting the worst-case scenario to happen, no matter how unlikely it is
cbt.overgeneralization_desc: Making broad interpretations from a single or few events
cbt.mind_reading_desc: Assuming you know what others are thinking without evidence
cbt.emotional_reasoning_desc: Believing that your negative emotions reflect the way things really are
cbt.should_statements_desc: Using 'should' or 'must' statements that create unrealistic expectations
cbt.personalization_desc: Blaming yourself for events outside your control
cbt.what_if_thinking_desc: Constantly asking 'what if' questions that fuel anxiety
cbt.labeling_desc: Assigning negative labels to yourself or others based on specific behaviors
cbt.connect_thought_journal_description: Connect this log to a thought journal entry to explore the thoughts and emotions behind your feelings. This can help you identify patterns and develop better coping strategies
cbt.edit_connection_description: This log is already connected to a thought journal entry. You can edit the existing entry or keep it as is
